extra.reasoning: Property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50): IF (bradycardia AND low_ejection_fraction AND hyperkalemia). rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50): IF bradycardia. The condition for rec_drug_a is a strict superset of (and thus implies) the condition for rec_drug_b, since both require bradycardia. There is no rule that sets rec_drug_a:=false or rec_drug_b:=false; the default only applies when an output is left unset, and here both outputs are set by their matching rules. With bradycardia=true, low_ejection_fraction=true, hyperkalemia=true: rec_a_from_conjunction fires (rec_drug_a=true) and rec_b_from_trigger fires (rec_drug_b=true). No priority or ordering resolves this because the two outputs are independent; priorities only resolve competing rules on the SAME output, and each output has just one rule writing true. heart_failure and prior_gi_bleed are irrelevant to a/b (they only affect rec_drug_c) and are set false. Hence both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are jointly true, violating mutual exclusion.
